title: Fix Claude MCP config path: .mcp.json + scope flag + deep-merge + doctor migration
description: config/json.ts:99-109 currently writes mcpServers.fabric to .claude/settings.json (which is for hooks/permissions, NOT MCP servers — NONCOMPLIANT with Claude Code spec). Rewrite to write to project-scope .mcp.json (default) or user-scope ~/.claude.json based on --scope flag (D11). Interactive prompt if flag absent. Deep-merge via lodash.merge (Q6) to preserve other mcpServers entries (D12). Doctor check `mcp_config_in_wrong_file` fixable: --fix removes stale settings.json mcpServers.fabric entry + writes mcp_config_migrated ledger event (D13). Snapshot test matches Claude Code MCP spec.
scope: Layer 2 P0 — Claude Code spec compliance; the most surprising bug surfaced by domain analysis

files.change: Rewrite Claude MCP config: emit to .mcp.json (project) or ~/.claude.json (user) based on scope; deep-merge via lodash.merge to preserve other mcpServers entries; remove old settings.json write path
Add --scope project|user CLI flag (default project per D11). Interactive prompt when flag absent: 'Install Fabric MCP at project scope (.mcp.json) or user scope (~/.claude.json)?'
New fixable check `mcp_config_in_wrong_file` detecting mcpServers.fabric in .claude/settings.json. --fix removes the entry + writes mcp_config_migrated ledger event
Add lodash.merge to dependencies (or workspace root)
NEW: snapshot for .mcp.json with fabric entry alongside hypothetical other-server entry; verifies deep-merge preserves other entries
NEW: 4 fixture cases (empty / fabric-only / other-mcp-only / fabric+other) verify lodash.merge preserves all entries

criteria: fab init --scope project writes .mcp.json (creates if absent, deep-merges if exists)
doctor detects mcpServers.fabric in settings.json and offers --fix
--fix removes settings.json entry + writes mcp_config_migrated event
Snapshot test matches Claude Code MCP spec format
Tests pass in same commit; existing init flow tests still pass
